media.title:
Every dress starts here.

Pencil on paper. A neckline drawn three times until the line sits right. A skirt that won't be a skirt until we agree on the way it should fall.

Some of these sketches make it to a pattern. Some don't. The ones that do, we sit with for months.  Fabric chosen, samples cut, then re-cut, then re-cut again. A tie front that needed to be a fraction shorter. A hem that wanted to be lace, not piping.

The finished dresses you see on the site don't look like much in pencil. But every one of them was a piece of paper first, on a table, with someone deciding it was worth making.

Designed slowly. Worn often.

#designprocess #slowfashion #behindthebrand #meandlia

title:
Some dresses earn their keep. Worn to the harbor, the dinner, the trip, and the quiet Tuesday that turns into something.

#slowfashion #premiumcotton #meandlia #slowwardrobe #naturalfabrics

media.title:
Some things take a season. Ours took years.

A lot of coffee, a lot of conversation, a lot of cutting up fabric samples on the floor. Choosing one cotton over another because the hand of it just felt right. Sending a pattern back because the hem wasn't sitting the way we wanted.

We're slow. On purpose.

While most brands chase the next drop, we sit with one piece until it's something we'd actually want to wear for years. Fabrics that breathe. Lace trimmed by hand. Seams that hold their shape after a hundred wears.

It's a strange way to run a fashion brand. But fast fashion is designed to be replaced, and we'd rather make things that get kept.

A small rebellion against a rushed life.

#slowfashion #handmade #naturalfibres #meandlia

A short letter about scrunchies.
Every first dress we send out comes with one â cut from the same cotton, tied around the tissue by hand before it ships.
It started as a way to use the offcuts, so nothing good went to waste. It's become the thing people message us about most.
Yours either way. Whether the dress stays or not.

#slowfashion #madebyhand #meandlia

media.title:
People ask where the name comes from.

Me + Lia isn't a marketing word. It's the two of us.

Melina and Meilia. A daughter and her mum. Put the names together and you get Me + Lia.  The way Melina tells it, it's also "just me, and my mum." Both feel right.

We started this in 2022 because we were tired of the same thing. Clothes made to be seen once and forgotten. Fast, loud, gone by the next season. Beautiful in the photo, disappointing in the hand.

So we made the opposite. A small number of pieces, in cotton and linen, designed slowly and meant to be worn for years. The kind of things you reach for without thinking, because they're soft, and they're yours.

That's the whole idea. A mother and a daughter, making things we'd actually want to live in.

No notes.

#meandlia #slowfashion #motherdaughter #thoughtfulpieces
